dataset: NOAA Mauna Loa CO2 Monthly Mean

pi_items.source: Task description and export_result.sh confirm this is the expected output filename
URL successfully accessed and confirmed to contain real NOAA Mauna Loa CO2 data. File created 2026-02-05 by NOAA GML.
Downloaded and counted actual data rows from NOAA CSV file (excluding header comments)
Calculated decimal year for May 15, 2030: (134 days from start of year / 365 days) + 2030 = 2030.367. May 2030 spans approximately 2030.333 to 2030.417.

Linear regression on NOAA data (2005-2026, 20 years): slope = 2.359 ppm/year. Projection for May 2030.375: 437.28 ppm. Alternative fit (2015-2026, 10 years): slope = 2.506 ppm/year, projects to 438.82 ppm. Metaculus community forecast for 2030 is 437 ppm.

Linear regression using last 10 years of data (faster growth rate): projects to 438.82 ppm for May 2030. The range 438-455 ppm is wider than single-model projections but accounts for uncertainty. Value 455 ppm appears higher than expected from linear trend but could represent upper uncertainty bound.
Verified in official GeoGebra Manual (wiki.geogebra.org and geogebra.github.io/docs/manual). FitPoly: polynomial regression. FitExp: exponential regression (a*e^(bx)). FitSin: sine wave fitting.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly-available NOAA Mauna Loa CO2 monthly data (815 data points from 1958-2026). The data URL is correct and current as of February 2026. The decimal date 2030.375 correctly represents May 2030. Based on linear regression of recent NOAA data (2005-2026), CO2 levels in May 2030 are projected to reach approximately 437-439 ppm, placing the metadata minimum of 438 ppm at the lower edge of predictions. The maximum of 455 ppm exceeds linear trend projections but may represent an uncertainty bound. All required GeoGebra commands (FitPoly, FitExp, FitSin) are real and documented. The task data is real, not synthetic.
